name: Cherry Pinwheel Slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the flour and salt in a medium bowl. Cut in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
2. Add the sour cream; stir until blended.
3. Divide the dough into 4 pieces. Wrap each piece separately in plastic wrap or waxed paper.
4. Refrigerate for 2-4 hours.
5. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
6. Roll out one piece of dough at a time on a lightly floured surface into a 12x6 inches rectangle.
7. Spread one fourth of the filling over the dough and sprinkle with 1/4 cup coconut and 1/4 cup pecans.
8. Roll up, jelly-roll style, starting from the short side. Pinch the seam to seal.
9. Place the roll, seam side down, on an ungreased baking sheet.
10. Repeat steps 6-9 with the remaining dough, filling, coconut, and pecans.
11. Bake for 40-45 minutes or until the rolls are golden brown. Remove from baking sheets to wire racks.
12. Dust generously with confectioners' sugar while still warm. Cool completely.
13. Cut into 1-inch slices.
Makes about 2 dozen cookies.
